db.lua:28 — assertion `#l == 0` fails because the line-hook callback fired (or recorded events) when it should not have. Test pattern: `debug.sethook(f, 'l')` then `load(s)()` then `debug.sethook()`. Look at `trace_exec` line-hook dispatch around line ~1916 in crates/lua-vm/src/debug.rs and the recently-added C-frame guard. The guard may be too lax. Scope: make ONLY this assertion at db.lua:28 pass; do not refactor the wider debug subsystem. Verify by running `./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/db.lua` from project root. Then confirm no regression by re-running the smoke set: `for t in strings closure tracegc big sort math; do ./harness/run_one_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/$t.lua; done`.
Re-run db.lua oracle after fix-db-line-hook-timing lands. Records pass/fail to the evidence ledger.

gc.lua TIMES OUT in the 'self-referenced threads' section. The GC walk does not converge on cycles. Look at the trace fixed-point in crates/lua-gc/src/heap.rs and the `impl Trace for GlobalState` thread-visiting block in crates/lua-vm/src/trace_impls.rs. Likely fix: in the post-mark fixed-point hook, detect when nothing new is marked in an iteration and exit, plus correctly drain `twups` / `cross_thread_upvals` mirrors so the cycle resolves. PRE-FLIGHT CHECK (required): run `./harness/canaries/gc/run_gc_canaries.sh` BEFORE editing — capture baseline. The canary `canary_b_coro_upvalue.lua` is the diagnostic mode you need to keep green. Verify by running `./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/gc.lua` — must complete within 5 minutes. Then re-run GC canaries to confirm no regression.
Re-run gc.lua oracle after fix-gc-cycle-fixed-point lands. Records pass/fail to the evidence ledger.
all.lua is a composite test downstream of gc.lua. Should flip to PASS automatically once fix-gc-cycle-fixed-point lands. No standalone agent packet needed.

gengc.lua:130 — real GC barrier issue (verified NOT a generational-GC bug; reproduces under incremental mode via `harness/canaries/gc/canary_b_coro_upvalue.lua`). Current barriers in crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs:2789-2806 are empty `{}` bodies. `keep_invariant()` and `is_sweep_phase()` always return `false`. Reference: `lgc.c::luaC_barrier`, `lgc.c::luaC_barrierback`, `lgc.h::keepinvariant` / `issweepphase` macros. Retires the `gc-barrier-noops` and `gc-phase-predicates-always-constant` ghost entries in docs/GHOST_ABSTRACTION_REGISTER.md — update that file when done. PRE-FLIGHT (required): run `./harness/canaries/gc/run_gc_canaries.sh` and capture baseline. Verify: `./harness/run_official_test.sh reference/lua-c/testes/gengc.lua` and re-run GC canaries.
Re-run gengc.lua oracle after fix-gengc-barrier-noops lands.

coroutine.lua:327 — yield-through-pcall. C-Lua reference: `ldo.c::lua_resume` lines ~575-625 (`if (s == LUA_YIELD) finishCcall(L, ci);`). The C-Lua resume path through frames marked CIST_YPCALL is incomplete; F-3.a landed `pcall_k` yieldable branch, the resume side that continues those frames via `finishCcall(L, ci)` needs work. CRITICAL SCOPE: previous agents stalled chasing this with too-broad scope (errfunc=5 save/restore across xpcall). Scope to ONE assertion first — flip the `coroutine.lua:282-289` subtest only; the surrounding test code already passes. If that single subtest passes, stop and let a separate packet handle deeper assertions. selector=manual to avoid auto-dispatching this until simpler packets land.
Re-run coroutine.lua oracle after fix-coroutine-yield-through-pcall lands.

locals.lua:982 — yield inside `__close` metamethod. Test pattern: `__close` does `coroutine.yield(...)` then returns. Needs the CIST_CLSRET callstatus flag wired and OP_CLOSE / OP_TBC paths to honor it on resume. Where to look: crates/lua-vm/src/vm.rs (OP_TBC, OP_CLOSE dispatch), crates/lua-vm/src/do_.rs (close_protected re-entry). CRITICAL SCOPE: prior agent stalled with too-broad scope. Target ONLY locals.lua:975-985 first; do not refactor wider close/tbc handling. selector=manual to avoid auto-dispatching until simpler packets land.
Re-run locals.lua oracle after fix-locals-yield-in-close lands.

PROFILE EVIDENCE: harness/bench/profiles/20260523T043338Z-408156e-table_ops_long/summary.txt shows api::index_to_value at 19.5% of wall-clock — called per get_i/set_i in the table.remove and table.insert shift loops. The same table value is being re-resolved from stack index 1 on every one of ~5000 iterations. HYPOTHESIS: cache the resolved LuaValue::Table at the top of table.remove/insert and call a state method that takes the table directly, bypassing index_to_value per iteration. Look at how C-Lua's tremove (ltablib.c) reads the table once via aux_getn's checktab and then uses lua_geti/lua_seti (which still re-resolve, but C's index lookup is a single pointer arithmetic, ours is a method call). The fix may be to add raw_seti_table_direct/raw_geti_table_direct that take &GcRef<LuaTable> instead of stack idx. EXPECTED IMPACT: table_ops 4.80x -> ~3.5x. Verify with `./harness/bench/compare.sh --runs 5`. 44/44 must stay green.

PROFILE EVIDENCE: table_ops_long profile shows TableInner::get_slot at 14.7% + slot_value at 12.4% = 27% combined. They are conceptually one operation (find slot, return value) but our impl splits them via TableSlotRef. C-Lua's luaH_getint returns the value directly from the array index — one memory load. HYPOTHESIS: add a direct LuaValue-returning fast path for the array-part case in get_int_slot, skipping the TableSlotRef indirection when the key is within alimit. Look at TableInner::get_int_slot in crates/lua-types/src/table.rs around line 730. The array path (uk.wrapping_sub(1) < alimit) can return the array[i] LuaValue directly without going through slot_value. EXPECTED IMPACT: table_ops should drop 0.5-1x. binarytrees may benefit too. Bench via compare.sh. 44/44 must stay green.

PROFILE EVIDENCE: fibonacci profile shows vm::execute at 69.9% — a tight bytecode loop. The OP_ADD/OP_SUB/OP_MUL handlers clone both operands before passing to arith_op_aux_rr to drop the borrow on `state`. C-Lua passes TValue pointers. We clone because of borrow checker accommodation, NOT safety. HYPOTHESIS: rewrite the arith handlers (Add/Sub/Mul) to pattern-match integer operands DIRECTLY from the stack via Option<i64>-returning accessors that don't clone the LuaValue enum. Pseudo: `if let (Some(i1), Some(i2)) = (state.get_int_at(rb), state.get_int_at(rc)) { state.set_at(ra, LuaValue::Int(i1.wrapping_add(i2))); } else { /* float/metamethod slow path with clones */ }`. Add get_int_at(idx) -> Option<i64> to LuaState that reads the stack slot tag without cloning. EXPECTED IMPACT: fibonacci 2.78x -> ~2.0x. mandelbrot may benefit similarly. Bench via compare.sh. 44/44 must stay green.

PROFILE EVIDENCE: fibonacci profile shows LuaState::upvalue_get at 8.8% of wall. Each call does 3 RefCell::borrow operations: cl.upvals[i].borrow(), uv.state.borrow() (via slot()), and self.global().current_thread_id (RefCell on global state). For single-threaded code (no coroutines being resumed), current_thread_id never changes during a call. HYPOTHESIS: cache current_thread_id as a regular field on LuaState that gets updated only on thread switch (which happens via lua-coro's resume). Then upvalue_get can read it without the RefCell borrow. Look at upvalue_get in crates/lua-vm/src/state.rs around line 1989. Also check whether the upvalue path can be split such that the open-upvalue + current-thread case (the common one) skips the cl.upvals[i].borrow().clone() in favor of a slice-index access. EXPECTED IMPACT: fibonacci 2.78x -> ~2.5x, closure_ops 2.28x -> ~1.8x. Bench via compare.sh. 44/44 must stay green.

EXISTING PERF(port) CALLOUT at crates/lua-vm/src/tagmethods.rs:328 says 'Vec allocation on every call — profile in Phase B; may become a Cow<\'static, [u8]> once lifetimes are firmed up'. obj_type_name returns Vec<u8> for the type name, allocating per call. The static type names (b"nil", b"boolean", b"number" etc.) can be returned as &'static [u8] without allocation. HYPOTHESIS: change obj_type_name to return Cow<'static, [u8]> — Borrowed for the static-name case (no metatable __name), Owned only when reading a custom __name from a metatable. Update callers to accept Cow or call .as_ref() at the use site. Look at TYPE_NAMES in the same file. EXPECTED IMPACT: small per-call savings, may show up indirectly. The point is to retire the PERF(port) callout. Bench via compare.sh. 44/44 must stay green.

PROFILE EVIDENCE: string_ops_long pre-fix profile (20260523T034951Z) showed 62% in _platform_memmove driven by to_lua_string_bytes copies. Commit 685482d fixed gmatch_aux. There may be similar patterns elsewhere in string_lib.rs. HYPOTHESIS: audit ALL functions in string_lib.rs that call to_lua_string_bytes or do .to_vec() on owned data. Convert hot paths to use state.to_lua_string(idx) -> Option<GcRef<LuaString>> and borrow .as_bytes() directly, holding the GcRef to keep the bytes alive. Candidates to inspect: str_match, str_find_aux, str_gsub, str_format, string.byte/char/sub/rep/reverse/upper/lower. EXPECTED IMPACT: string_ops 3.00x -> ~2.0x, string_ops_long similar. Bench via compare.sh. 44/44 must stay green.
